reproducible_artifact: A deploy-readiness checklist (PR template / merge-gate predicate) plus a paired sprint-planning displacement-detection question; together they encode all three prohibitions of the verse.
embodiment_sketch: Concrete artifact 1 — PR template field (deploy-readiness gate): "[ ] Code reviewed. [ ] Tests pass under CI. [ ] Rollback plan exists in runbook. [ ] Observability (Datadog dashboard + Sentry alert) is wired for the changed endpoint. If all four are checked, the merge proceeds; outcome uncertainty is not a fifth checkbox." This embodies the First/Second prohibitions: jurisdiction is the four boxes; outcome is not. Concrete artifact 2 — sprint-planning displacement-detection question (one line on the sprint review agenda): "For each in-flight ticket older than one week: is the work being done the customer-facing item that was queued, or has it become a comfortable internal rewrite? Comfort answer triggers a re-prioritization conversation." This embodies the Third prohibition (mā saṅgaḥ akarmaṇi). Concrete artifact 3 — post-launch readback (15-minute meeting agenda item, two days after every launch): "Read the Datadog dashboard and the Sentry error rate aloud. Decide: does the data change next week's plan? The decision to look at the data is mandatory; the data's verdict is not under your control." This embodies the falsifiability clause (detachment from outcome is not detachment from feedback).
verdict_reason: v3.1's engineering layer is concretely embodiable. The translation names code artifacts (function, migration, deploy, pipeline) and observability artifacts (Datadog, Sentry) that map directly to a merge-gate predicate. The falsifiability clause is itself almost-runbook ('shipping without reading Datadog dashboards, ignoring Sentry error reports, or skipping post-launch instrumentation' = explicit failure modes for a checklist). The implication ('Read your dashboards as honestly as you read your code reviews') is one inch from a meeting-agenda item. The tags include shipping-discipline and iteration-loop, which are structural rather than purely state-of-mind tags, satisfying the calibration heuristic about tag-mix. A senior engineer could add Artifacts 1, 2, and 3 to their team's actual practice on Monday without re-reading the Gita. PASS.
